div.head: The Acting Secretary of State to the Chairman of the American Delegation (Stimson)
div.p:
84. There was one other matter, in discussing your offer further with the President before his departure for Florida, concerning which you probably understand his wishes; at the risk, however, of repetition we again explain that 200,000 tons for destroyers seems to be a very high figure. Presumably it is now being placed that high by you so that negotiations between Great Britain and France may be left in
a better position; the President hopes, however, that 150,000 tons will be the limit for the final figure.
In regard to your offer, the above is the last comment. It appears to us here that your general strategy is correct; only those who will oppose whatever you do are opposed to your plan.
